title: Queues, Streams, Super Streams, Retention, and Replay · Narrowing Workshop
summary: A messaging pairing room predicts publisher, exchange, queue, consumer, ownership, and failure transitions before assembling the first queue-versus-stream workload decision and replay plan for queues, streams, super streams, retention, and replay. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of queue-versus-stream workload decision and replay plan while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, an…
objectives: Compare destructive queue consumption with retained stream offsets, replay, retention, protocol clients, and consumer responsibility.
Define age and byte retention, segment behavior, start offset, consumer progress, replay authorization, and late-reader expectations.
Use super streams to partition throughput by key while accounting for partitions, routing, per-partition order, skew, and consumer coordination.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Queue Stream Semantics
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop messaging team handles case 5687 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to choose queue, stream, or super stream from replay, backlog, throughput, and order-key evidence; prove topology rollback before deleting the old binding; then fail the canary and prove older consumers remain compatible during rollback. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare destructive queue consumption with retained stream offsets, replay, retention, protocol clients, and consumer responsibility.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Queue Stream Semantics.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compare destructive queue consumption with retained stream offsets, replay, retention, protocol clients, and consumer responsibility. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Queue Stream Semantics, keep Queue Stream Semantics connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

content.text: A workshop messaging team handles case 9639 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to choose queue, stream, or super stream from replay, backlog, throughput, and order-key evidence; prove topology rollback before deleting the old binding; then remove the dead-letter binding and expose the resulting retention risk. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
